summary: This technical post addresses the challenge of dynamically generating partitions for SSAS (SQL Server Analysis Services) Cubes, which lacks built-in easy partition management. The author presents a SQL-based solution that uses a linked server to execute XMLA statements from T-SQL, enabling automated monthly partition creation. The approach involves creating stored procedures that generate and execute XMLA commands to delete and recreate partitions based on date ranges. This allows for programmatic cube partition management without relying on SSIS packages.
key_points: SSAS lacks native easy partition generation, requiring either SSIS packages or custom SQL scripts
Creating a linked server enables executing XMLA statements through relational T-SQL
The solution uses stored procedures to dynamically generate monthly partitions with configurable date ranges
The script handles both initial 'before' partitions and iterative month-by-month partition creation
XMLA commands are constructed as XML strings and executed against the Analysis Services linked server
money_quotes: There is no easy way to generate partition for SSAS Cubes by default
The trick is, to create a linked server, that you can execute XMLA statements with relational T-SQL
key_insight: A linked server bridging T-SQL and XMLA enables automated SSAS cube partition management that would otherwise require complex SSIS packages.
